pattern: Treating every intermediate value x can pass through as a graph node connected by its four moves turns 'fewest operations' into an unweighted shortest path search from x to y.
hints: Every operation changes the current value to exactly one new value, so think of each reachable integer as a node and each operation as an edge of weight one.
Breadth-first search from the starting value explores values in order of operation count, so the first time you reach the target you have the minimum.
The search never needs to wander far past the larger of the two values plus a small buffer, since overshooting only helps when it immediately unlocks a division by 5 or 11.

edge_inputs.description: one division then a decrement gets there
target above start needs only increments
both values at the minimum of the range
both values at the maximum of the range
start is a large multiple of 11 but target is small and not a clean divisor chain
start below target where dividing would only move further away

stress.description: random pairs across the full range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    x = rng.randint(1, 10000)
    y = rng.randint(1, 10000)
    return [x, y]
stress.note: exercises the BFS frontier growth across the whole value range

stress.description: pairs near multiples of 5 and 11 where divisions chain
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    base = rng.choice([5, 11, 25, 55, 121, 605])
    x = max(1, min(10000, base * rng.randint(1, 20) + rng.randint(-3, 3)))
    y = rng.randint(1, 10000)
    return [x, y]
stress.note: stresses the division branches of the search
